subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: An insurance policy reimburses a loss up to a benefit limit of 10. The policyholder's loss, $X$, follows a distribution with density function: $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{2}{x^3} & x > 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ What is the expected value of the benefit paid under the insurance policy?
explanation: Let $Y$ denote the claim payments. Then $Y = \begin{cases} X & 1 < X \le 10 \\ 10 & X \ge 10 \end{cases}$ It follows that $E(Y) = \int_1^{10} x \cdot \frac{2}{x^3}\,dx + \int_{10}^\infty 10 \cdot \frac{2}{x^3}\,dx = -\frac{2}{x}\Big|_1^{10} - \frac{10}{x^2}\Big|_{10}^\infty = 1.9$.
